content: The entire Meta module (src/lib/meta/) ships DORMANT and fails safe OFF: isMetaEnabled() reads flags.meta_integration and coalesces a missing row, a disabled row, OR any read error to false; createMetaClient()/getMetaConfig() THROW when META_APP_ID/META_APP_SECRET are absent so no Graph call is ever attempted without creds. Callers hitting Graph must gate on BOTH isMetaConfigured() (env) AND await isMetaEnabled() (flag). App Review takes weeks (spec §11 R-6), so nothing lights up implicitly.

name: injectable-graph-client
description: Graph client makes base URL/version/fetch injectable so tests never hit the network; MetaApiError classifies auth/rate-limit errors for the CAPI worker

name: client-pixel-isolation
description: Client-side seller Meta Pixel (no OAuth/CAPI): reuse meta_connections (pixel_id + status=active, other cols null). R-4 isolation is a single data-layer chokepoint — getOwnerPixelId(db, listing.sellerId) indexes strictly by the current listing owner, so seller B's page can never resolve seller A's pixel; MetaPixel fires only that one id, gated on cookie consent + isValidPixelId. Re-verification confirmed: typecheck/lint/test(486)/next build all green on re-dispatch.

serialize() in src/lib/leads/types.ts deliberately DROPS meta_event_id from the LeadDTO (server-internal CAPI dedup key), so the inquiry POST response does NOT return leads.meta_event_id to the browser — contrary to task assumptions. Client Lead events cannot obtain the server-minted event_id without changing the leads-owned serializer.
resolution: Inquiry form reads data.lead?.metaEventId if present and falls back to letting Meta assign its own eventID; in the no-CAPI rescope dedup is moot. If a shared event_id is later required, expose metaEventId on LeadDTO (leads domain change, out of pixel scope).
